zh.Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England: 英國萊希斯特斯爾馬可特 博斯沃斯
zh.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Payne II, Knights Templar是Lady Elizabeth de Thoms的兒子，於1170年出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾馬可特 博斯沃斯。他和Lady Elizabeth Marguerite至少有2個兒子。他去世於1245年在他的家鄉享年75歲。

zh-hans.Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England: 英国萊希斯特斯爾馬可特 博斯沃斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Payne II, Knights Templar是Lady Elizabeth de Thoms的儿子，于1170年出生在英国萊希斯特斯爾馬可特 博斯沃斯。他和Lady Elizabeth Marguerite至少有2个儿子。他去世于1245年在他的家乡享年75岁。

nameMeanings.name: Payne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen (from Latin Paganus), a fairly common personal name among Normans. It derived from a word that originally meant ‘villager, rustic’, later ‘heathen’, but it had doubtless lost these connotations in its use as a late medieval personal name. This name has also been established in Ireland since the 14th century.

History: Thomas Payne, who was a freeman of Plymouth Colony in 1639, was the founder of a large American family, which included Robert Treat Paine (1731–1814), one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. See also   Paine  .

variants.name: Payn
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Payen
meanings: French: from the Old French personal name Pagen, Paien, from Latin Paganus, from paganus ‘country-dweller’, a derivative of pagus ‘of or relating to the countryside’, possibly given to a child not yet baptized (because of its later sense of ‘pagan, heathen’). Alternatively, a nickname for a country-born person. This surname is also found in the West Indies (mainly Haiti) and Mauritius.
English: variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Pagan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Juan, Angel, Carlos, Rafael, Ramon, Pedro, Francisco, Jesus, Miguel, Julio.

Spanish and Catalan: Castilianized form (Pagán) of Catalan Pagà, from the Late Latin personal name Paganus, which as a derivative of Latin pagus ‘outlying village’ originally meant ‘villager’ (see Payne ). Alternatively, in some cases, a topographic name from Catalan pagà ‘heath’ (from the same Latin word pagus).
English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen, from Late Latin Paganus (see Payne , compare 1 above).

variants.name: Paynes
meanings: English: variant of Payne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Paine
meanings: English: variant of   Payne  .

History: The author of the republican treatise The Rights of Man, Thomas Paine (1737–1809), left England for North America in the mid 1770s, where he became involved in the movement that led to independence. His pamphlet of 1776, Common Sense, influenced the Declaration of Independence and furnished some of the arguments justifying it.

variants.name: Pank
meanings: English (Norfolk): perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Pank(e). It could be a shortened form of Pancok, which is probably a pet form of Pane (see Payne ).
Sorbian: nickname from a diminutive of pan ‘mister, lord, landlord’ (see Pan ). Compare Panke .

variants.name: Pane
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Salvatore, Aldo, Carmela, Carmine, Ciro, Concetta, Enzo, Giuseppe, Mario, Armando, Claudio, Macario, Remigio.

Italian: from pane ‘bread’, used as a metonymic occupational name for a baker or as a nickname meaning ‘good thing’, and also as a personal name. This surname is also found in southern France and Corsica.
English: variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Panes
meanings: English (Somerset): variant of Payne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Pannell
meanings: English (southeastern):
possibly from Middle English panel, panel(l)e ‘cloth, saddle-cloth, saddle’ or ‘pane (of glass)’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of such items.
from the Middle English personal name Paynel (Old French Payenel, Latin Paganellus), a pet form of Payne (from Latin Paganus).

variants.name: Penson
meanings: English:
patronymic from the Middle English female personal name Penn, a pet form of Pernel, or perhaps from the Middle English personal name Payne + -son. See Penn , Parnell .

habitational name from Penson in North Huish (Devon), which derives from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Payn(e) (see Payne ) + Middle English toun ‘village, town, settlement’ (Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’).

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Payne II, Knights Templar was born in 1170, in Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England as the son of Lady Elizabeth de Thoms. He had at least 2 sons with Lady Elizabeth Marguerite. He died in 1245, in his hometown, at the age of 75.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Payne II, Knights Templar est né en 1170, à Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, Angleterre, fils de Lady Elizabeth de Thoms. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Lady Elizabeth Marguerite. Il est décédé en 1245, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 75 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Payne II, Knights Templar wurde 1170 in Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England, als Sohn von Lady Elizabeth de Thoms geboren. Er hatte mit Lady Elizabeth Marguerite mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1245 im Alter von 75 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Payne II, Knights Templar è nato il nel 1170 nel Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England come figlio di Lady Elizabeth de Thoms. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Lady Elizabeth Marguerite. Morì nel 1245, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 75 anni.

ja.Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England: イギリスレアイセステルシュアイアマーケト ボスウオルス
ja.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Payne II, Knights Templarは1170年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアマーケト ボスウオルスでLady Elizabeth de Thomsの息子として生まれた。彼はLady Elizabeth Margueriteとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼は1245年に故郷で75歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Payne II, Knights Templar(은)는 1170년 영국 레이스스터실르 마크트 보스워스에서 Lady Elizabeth de Thoms의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Lady Elizabeth Marguerite(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1245년 그의 고향에서 75세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Payne II, Knights Templar nasceu em 1170, em Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, Inglaterra filho de Lady Elizabeth de Thoms. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Lady Elizabeth Marguerite. Ele faleceu em 1245, em sua cidade natal, com 75 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Payne II, Knights Templar родился в 1170 г. в местности маркет босворт, лисестиршир, Англия. Его мать звали Lady Elizabeth de Thoms. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Lady Elizabeth Marguerite. Он умер в 1245 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 75 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Payne II, Knights Templar nació en 1170, en Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, Inglaterra como hijo de Lady Elizabeth de Thoms. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Lady Elizabeth Marguerite. Murió en 1245, en su pueblo, a la edad de 75 años.
